refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement period and baseline, (2) what constitutes 'available' vs. 'unavailable' (e.g., does safety shutdown count as unavailability?), (3) the scope of the system (vehicle only, or vehicle + teleoperation + support services), and (4) the role of planned vs. unplanned downtime. High safety criticality means availability must not be achieved by deferring safety actions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational logs (uptime/downtime events) across the evaluation period; categorize by root cause (mechanical failure, software fault, planned maintenance, safety intervention, network outage); calculate availability ratio; compare against 0.97 target and customer-defined acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.97 availability by minimizing unplanned mechanical and electrical failures.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall achieve ≥0.97 availability by maintaining reliable network connectivity and command/response latency within operational limits.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall achieve ≥0.97 availability by ensuring safety monitoring, alerting, and intervention mechanisms remain operational without false negatives.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall integrate availability data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to report composite system availability and identify bottlenecks.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundancy (dual motors, dual network links, dual safety controllers) to mask single-point failures and maintain availability during component maintenance or failure.

DP-B: Implement predictive maintenance scheduling (condition monitoring, prognostic algorithms) to detect degradation early and schedule maintenance during planned downtime windows, reducing unplanned failures.
DP-C: Implement fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) logic in Vehicle and Safety Services to automatically transition to safe degraded modes without human intervention.
DP-D: Implement real-time availability monitoring and reporting dashboard that tracks uptime/downtime events, root causes, and alerts stakeholders to emerging availability risks.
DP-E: Implement a combination of redundancy (DP-A), predictive maintenance (DP-B), FDIR (DP-C), and monitoring (DP-D) as an integrated availability assurance strategy.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-E: Implement an integrated availability assurance strategy combining: (1) selective redundancy for critical safety-related subsystems (dual safety controllers, dual network links for teleoperation); (2) condition-based predictive maintenance for mechanical/electrical components (vibration monitoring, thermal monitoring, battery health); (3) automated fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) in Vehicle and Safety Services to transition to safe degraded modes; (4) real-time availability monitoring and reporting via a centralized dashboard accessible to Site Manager, Maintenance, and System Engineer, with alerts triggered when availability trends fall below 0.97 or when critical components approach end-of-life.

potential_sources: Redundancy design (DP-A) couples Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems (both require dual network links); mitigation: use standardized interface (Network Link) to decouple physical implementation.
Predictive maintenance (DP-B) couples Vehicle and Maintenance/Quality stakeholder; mitigation: define clear data contracts (Telemetry interface) for condition data export.
FDIR logic (DP-C) couples Safety Services and Vehicle; mitigation: use SafetyAlert interface to decouple decision logic from execution.
Monitoring dashboard (DP-D) couples all subsystems; mitigation: use standardized Telemetry interface for data collection.
coupling_check.notes: DP-E is moderately coupled but couplings are manageable via interface standardization. The integrated strategy is justified because availability is a system-level property that cannot be achieved by optimizing subsystems in isolation.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Customer-defined availability target is 0.97; if higher (e.g., 0.99), redundancy and FDIR complexity increase significantly.
ASSUMPTION: Planned maintenance windows are available and do not count against availability; if not, availability target becomes infeasible.
ASSUMPTION: Network infrastructure (for Teleoperation) is stable; if network availability is <0.97, system availability cannot exceed network availability.
RISK: Redundancy increases cost, weight, and complexity; cost-benefit analysis required.
RISK: Predictive maintenance algorithms require historical failure data; if unavailable, algorithms may be ineffective initially.
RISK: FDIR logic may introduce new failure modes (e.g., false positives triggering unnecessary safe shutdowns); validation required.
RISK: Safety criticality means availability must not be achieved by deferring safety actions; FDIR logic must be validated to ensure it does not mask safety hazards.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify with customer: (1) Is 0.97 availability measured over calendar time or operational time? (2) Do planned maintenance windows count as unavailability? (3) Do safety-driven shutdowns count as unavailability? (4) What is the acceptable cost/weight/complexity trade-off for redundancy? (5) Is network infrastructure availability guaranteed to be ≥0.97? Answers will refine DP-E and may require sub-DPs for each sub-FR.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and monitoring/reporting capabilities. It is responsible for: (1) defining and enforcing availability targets across all subsystems, (2) collecting and aggregating availability metrics from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, (3) providing a unified availability dashboard and alerting mechanism, (4) coordinating redundancy and FDIR strategies across subsystems, (5) managing the integration of predictive maintenance data into maintenance scheduling.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry from Vehicle (operational status, sensor readings, fault codes) and Teleoperation (network latency, link status, command/response times) and Safety Services (safety monitor status, alert history). Used to compute availability metrics and detect degradation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outbound tasking to Vehicle and Teleoperation to trigger maintenance actions, FDIR transitions, or safe shutdowns based on availability monitoring and predictive maintenance algorithms.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety alerts: inbound from Safety Services (safety hazards, intervention triggers); outbound from NGHE Solution to Safety Services (availability-driven safety constraints, e.g., 'do not operate if redundancy is lost').

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connectivity for teleoperation and remote monitoring. Availability of Network Link is a critical dependency for Teleoperation subsystem availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for availability assurance strategy. It owns the monitoring dashboard, availability metrics aggregation, and cross-subsystem coordination. Minimal diffusion: only the FDIR logic and redundancy control logic are distributed to Vehicle and Safety Services (necessary for real-time response), but the strategy and metrics are centralized.

Vehicle owns mechanical/electrical redundancy (dual motors, dual power supplies) and condition monitoring sensors (vibration, thermal). However, Vehicle does not own the predictive maintenance algorithms, FDIR decision logic, or availability reporting—these belong to NGHE Solution. Assigning DP-E entirely to Vehicle would create high diffusion and poor separation of concerns.

Teleoperation owns network redundancy (dual links) and network monitoring. However, Teleoperation does not own the overall availability strategy, predictive maintenance, or FDIR logic. Assigning DP-E to Teleoperation would create high diffusion and poor alignment with the system-level nature of availability.

Safety Services owns safety monitoring and alerting. FDIR logic for safety-critical transitions (e.g., safe shutdown) may be co-located in Safety Services. However, Safety Services does not own the overall availability strategy, predictive maintenance, or cross-subsystem monitoring. Assigning DP-E to Safety Services would create high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-E (integrated availability assurance strategy) is best contained in NGHE Solution because: (1) availability is a system-level property that requires cross-subsystem visibility and coordination, (2) the monitoring dashboard, metrics aggregation, and alerting mechanism are naturally system-level concerns, (3) the predictive maintenance scheduling and FDIR decision logic require a holistic view of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services states, (4) NGHE Solution can delegate specific implementations (e.g., redundancy hardware in Vehicle, network monitoring in Teleoperation, safety FDIR in Safety Services) while retaining ownership of the overall strategy and metrics. This minimizes diffusion and maintains clear separation of concerns.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution is not given sufficient authority to enforce availability targets across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, the integrated strategy may fail. Mitigation: define clear ownership and decision rights in the system architecture.

RISK: If the monitoring dashboard and metrics aggregation are not implemented with sufficient real-time performance, availability reporting may lag behind actual system state. Mitigation: define latency and update frequency requirements for Telemetry interface.

RISK: If predictive maintenance algorithms are not validated against historical failure data, they may be ineffective or trigger false positives. Mitigation: establish a data collection and validation plan early in development.
RISK: If FDIR logic is not properly validated, it may introduce new failure modes or mask safety hazards. Mitigation: conduct formal safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) on FDIR logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm with stakeholders that NGHE Solution has the authority and resources to implement the integrated availability assurance strategy. Define clear interfaces and data contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services for: (1) telemetry data collection (Telemetry interface), (2) maintenance tasking (Tasking interface), (3) safety constraints (SafetyAlert interface), (4) network status (Network Link interface). Establish a governance model for availability target setting, monitoring, and escalation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-E (integrated availability assurance strategy) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability) through four complementary mechanisms: (1) redundancy masks single-point failures, (2) predictive maintenance reduces unplanned downtime, (3) FDIR enables automatic recovery without human intervention, (4) monitoring provides visibility and early warning. The DP is logically sound and feasible. No coupling issues that would prevent implementation. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: FDIR logic is distributed across Vehicle and Safety Services for real-time response, but the decision framework and metrics are centralized in NGHE Solution. This is acceptable because it balances real-time performance with architectural clarity.

Redundancy hardware (dual motors, dual power supplies) is owned by Vehicle, but redundancy strategy and switching logic are owned by NGHE Solution. This is acceptable because it maintains clear separation of concerns.
Predictive maintenance algorithms are owned by NGHE Solution, but condition monitoring sensors are owned by Vehicle. This is acceptable because the Telemetry interface provides a clean data contract.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for DP-E because it owns the system-level availability strategy, monitoring, and coordination. Diffusion is minimized by delegating specific implementations to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services while retaining ownership of the overall strategy. The four known interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) provide clean boundaries for subsystem integration.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible via operational testing: collect uptime/downtime logs over the agreed evaluation period, categorize by root cause, calculate availability ratio, and compare against 0.97 target. Verification of DP-E sub-components is also feasible: (1) redundancy can be verified via failure injection testing, (2) predictive maintenance can be verified via historical data analysis and algorithm validation, (3) FDIR can be verified via fault injection and recovery testing, (4) monitoring can be verified via dashboard accuracy and latency testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the overall availability assurance strategy and metrics; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own specific implementations (redundancy, network monitoring, safety FDIR) and report telemetry to NGHE Solution. The four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) provide a clear decomposition path for the next level of design.

recommended_revisions: Clarify with customer the definition of 'availability' (calendar time vs. operational time, treatment of planned maintenance and safety shutdowns) before finalizing DP-E.
Conduct a cost-benefit analysis of redundancy options (dual motors, dual power supplies, dual network links) to ensure the selected approach is economically justified.
Establish a data collection and validation plan for predictive maintenance algorithms early in development.
Conduct formal safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) on FDIR logic to ensure it does not introduce new failure modes or mask safety hazards.
Define clear ownership and decision rights for availability target enforcement across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs and sub-DPs for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The refined FR and selected DP-E are sound and pass the gate review, but they are not atomic and require further refinement at the next level. The four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) provide a clear decomposition path. Proceeding to the next level will enable detailed design of redundancy, predictive maintenance, FDIR, and monitoring mechanisms.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.97 availability by minimizing unplanned mechanical and electrical failures through redundancy, condition monitoring, and predictive maintenance.

FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall achieve ≥0.97 availability by maintaining reliable network connectivity and command/response latency within operational limits through redundant network links and network monitoring.

FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall achieve ≥0.97 availability by ensuring safety monitoring, alerting, and intervention mechanisms remain operational without false negatives through redundant safety controllers and automated FDIR.

FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall integrate availability data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to report composite system availability, identify bottlenecks, and trigger maintenance actions via a real-time monitoring dashboard.

assumptions: Customer-defined availability target is 0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period.
Planned maintenance windows are available and do not count against availability.
Network infrastructure (for Teleoperation) is stable and achieves ≥0.97 availability.
Historical failure data is available or will be collected during early operation to train predictive maintenance algorithms.
Safety criticality means availability must not be achieved by deferring safety actions; FDIR logic will be validated to ensure it does not mask safety hazards.
NGHE Solution has the authority and resources to enforce availability targets across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
open_risks: If customer-defined availability target is higher than 0.97 (e.g., 0.99), redundancy and FDIR complexity increase significantly, potentially exceeding cost/weight/complexity budgets.
If network infrastructure availability is <0.97, system availability cannot exceed network availability; mitigation requires network infrastructure upgrades outside the scope of NGHE Solution.
If predictive maintenance algorithms are not validated against historical failure data, they may be ineffective or trigger false positives, reducing availability instead of improving it.
If FDIR logic is not properly validated, it may introduce new failure modes (e.g., false positives triggering unnecessary safe shutdowns) or mask safety hazards.
If NGHE Solution is not given sufficient authority to enforce availability targets, the integrated strategy may fail due to subsystem conflicts or misaligned incentives.
evidence_needed: Customer acceptance criteria for availability (definition of 'available' vs. 'unavailable', treatment of planned maintenance and safety shutdowns, evaluation period).
Cost-benefit analysis of redundancy options (dual motors, dual power supplies, dual network links) to justify the selected approach.
Historical failure data for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to validate predictive maintenance algorithms.
Formal safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) on FDIR logic to ensure it does not introduce new failure modes or mask safety hazards.
Network infrastructure availability assessment to confirm it meets ≥0.97 target.
Governance model and decision rights for availability target enforcement across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
